title: Czech names and familiar forms
summary: Recognize common Czech first names and the familiar forms used constantly in ordinary conversation.
objectives: Match selected formal names with familiar forms
Recognize that a familiar form may be longer or look different
Avoid assuming every Czech name maps directly to English

sections.heading: Czech and English counterparts
sections.body: Some Czech names resemble English forms closely, while others have traditional counterparts that are less obvious.

sections.heading: Familiar names
sections.body: Czech uses familiar name forms frequently. Some are shorter, some are longer, and some differ substantially from the formal name.

resources.title: Original lesson: Names and Familiar Names
resources.note: Contains a larger table of common names and familiar forms.

exercises.prompt: Which familiar name commonly corresponds to Jan?
exercises.explanation: Honza is a familiar form of Jan.

exercises.prompt: Type a common familiar form of Kateřina.
exercises.explanation: Katka is a common familiar form.
Jiří commonly has the familiar form Jirka.
